title: Continuous Casting Machine (CCM)
author: JSL EKOS
description: This module explains the Continuous Casting Machine (CCM), where refined molten steel is transformed into semi-finished products such as slabs through a continuous and controlled solidification process.
learningObjectives: Understand the purpose of Continuous Casting.
Learn how molten steel solidifies into slabs.
Identify the major components of a CCM.

content.introduction: Continuous Casting is the process of converting refined molten steel into semi-finished products such as slabs. Instead of pouring steel into individual molds, the molten steel solidifies continuously, improving productivity and product quality.
content.purpose: The primary purpose of CCM is to continuously solidify molten steel into slabs with uniform dimensions and internal quality for further rolling operations.
content.workingPrinciple: Molten steel flows from the ladle into the tundish and then into a water-cooled copper mold. As the steel moves downward, it solidifies while being continuously withdrawn by rollers and cooled using secondary water sprays.
processSteps: Receive refined molten steel from LRF.
Transfer molten steel into the ladle.
Control steel flow through the submerged nozzle.
Solidify steel inside the copper mold.
content.summary: The Continuous Casting Machine converts refined molten steel into high-quality slabs through controlled solidification. Proper process control ensures defect-free products and supports efficient downstream rolling operations.
